text:
I didn't know I could not sub out toast for pancakes on the Triple Egg Dare Ya breakfast meal, said that's the way it came... yup, was told it would confuse the cooks.. definitely don't want to go to a place where the cooks get confused so easily. 

We Learned that the District manager was there, so we asked to speak to him on this crazy policy... Trina the manager came out instead of Jeff (the district manager) must of realized this is a stupid policy and sent Trina out-to deal with the problems of our visit today ... by the way,  Brooke our server, was great and was just doing what she was told. 

On top of that, our eggs (that were ordered over medium), came out runny. Our hash brown potatoes, which we ordered crispy, came kind of crispy on one side only, and my toast was barley toasted. Hey at least the coffee was hot. 

As we left to pay, Trina was at the register, and was apologetic, but mentioned that they would have allowed the substitution of meat for the pancakes... really!?! 
Isn't it easier to drop toast in a toaster then cook up meat or even grill the pancakes?

We should of went to our favorite breakfasts place, Benedict's, you can get what ever you like made fresh and is much better in quality. 

Sorry Perkins, you don't have such an awesome reputation where you can say "this is the only way we serve it ... take it or leave it".

- No Soup for You!

.
label: negative

text: I usually don't leave reviews.... But I find myself wanting to today.  The 1st positive is the hostess in the front was very friendly. The 1st negative is the coffee was bitter and barely warm. text:
What a cute place! It's very colorful inside and they have a lot of artwork on the walls. Every wall, corner, area seems to have its own 'theme.' The corner table we sat at, the walls and shelves were decorated with pigs! Everything was adorable. As for the food, I went for the lunch combination with a half sandwich and soup. I couldn't decide! Everything sounded so good, but I ended up getting the Spinach Melt and the Tomato Basil soup. I think the soup is their specialty since it's always on the menu, but they also have a soup of the day written on a chalkboard. I went with my coworkers and we all did half sandwich + the same tomato soup. Everyone loved the soup. It was very delicious. My coworker and I both got the Spinach Melt and we both liked it. Another got the Egg Salad and she loved it as well. It's her second time ordering it and she liked it both times so yay for consistency. She said she likes it because you can really taste the dill so if you're not a big fan of dill, keep that in mind! The Spinach Melt seems so easy to make, yet, my coworker and I both knew it would not taste as good at home. 

Overall, it feels very comfort food-y and feels like home (taking the words of my coworker). I'd like to bring my friends next time to try other things on the menu.
label: positive

text: If you wanna rich creamy coffee, say no more! The coffee here is so over the top, you have to come. I love to have a coffee that's so strong yet without bitterness; this place definitely serves it right. Not to mention they really listen to you while they're brewing, so if you have a request the barista's right there. And I have one pet peeve with most coffee shops... There's never any were comfortable to sit. Most coffee shops cater to the people who are studying or using their laptops; but I like to come with a friend and just cozy up on the couch for some good conversation and warm brew. Once again, this place delivers! Lots of nice cozy corners to snuggle into with your coffee. The inside is big and beautiful and has places for everybody to find the right place to enjoy. I'm certainly putting this one on my bookmarks; and I highly recommend them to anyone who's a coffee fanatic like me.

text: I don't know how to review this gas station worse than I am about to and I just want to say that I downloaded yelp specifically to warn you all. So the first time I came here I thought maybe it was a fluke. I went to one pump tried to pay by my card and the pump said to see the cashier. So I'm stubborn and just decided to drive around to another pump and use a different card. Same thing happens so I walk in and go to pay and the cashier says yea our pumps never work. So I chalk this up as a random occurrence. Well today my husband went to the same gas station and same thing happens to him so he goes inside says "please put 30 on pump___" goes back out and the station pumps three dollars and then stops so he goes back inside and tells the cashier- "hey I thought I said put thirty on pump ____" and the cashier goes oh yea sorry about that I'll do it now. So husband goes back out to the pump- still not working waits goes back inside and asks the cashier what the deal is. So then the cashier says oh... I thought you said $3... all I'm saying is save yourself some time and annoyance and go either a street up or somewhere else because this gas station will just give you problems... and you always have to go inside to pay. Your girls in a rush sometimes I just want to pump my gas and go.

text:
Right across from our hotel and we were starving after a long day of checking out the city. Reviews were decent so we wanted to try. Ordered the Rotolo (pic looks cool on website), Escarole Caesar, and a large Margarita pie.  Server asked if we wanted anchovies on the salad, and even though my daughter ordered it, I said sure. I love a couple of anchovies on a Caesar salad, so I thought if I snuck a bite, bonus for me. Server came back - no Rotolo.  Ok - so we ordered the breadsticks.  Breadsticks and salad arrived.  Breadsticks - no dipping sauce, kind of a disappointment - pretty good flavor from obviously their dough, but nothing more than Olive Garden really.  Caesar salad - wildly underdressed, so we asked for more dressing.  More dressing added, and completely overloaded with anchovies. Again, I love them, but so anchovy forward that if you like a good Caesar salad, this was nothing like that. It was kind of disgusting.  I am pretty sure that Caesar Salad is not native to Italy.  This should flat out be removed from their menu unless they change the recipe.  Lemon or salt would go miles to help this.  We got the "metro" size pizza as we were hungry; presentation 10/10, price 2/10 (I think it was $28 for a marginally sized flatbread), crust 10/10 (great chew and char), sauce 2/10 (prob San Marzano, but salt and olive oil were nonexistent), cheese 1/10 (almost non existent), and a handful of fresh basil thrown around on the flatbread.  
Service was pretty much as nonexistent as the flavor on the pie - we had to flag someone down if we wanted something.  I wouldn't be inclined to write this review if one person asked how our food was - no one did.  Seemed like a healthy takeout business which is good for them.  However, if you're trying to pick back up, customer service goes a long way.
Service - 1/10
Food  - 3/10
Overall - 3/10
Chance of returning - 0%
label: negative

text:
I Am A Biscuit Slut for Willa Jean - direct quote from our dope waitress who shall remain nameless (she knows who she is!). 

This spot hits for sure. We love another well no in place, also in the French quarter but we wanted to try something different and we are so glad we did! Walked in before I got too busy and we were seated immediately after they checked our vaccine cards. The menu is not vast but the items listed demands your attention. The first day we went, I ordered the hangover bowl and my wife had the avocado toast, preluded by the monkey bread and artichoke dip. All were absolutely fantastic. The monkey bread has crunchy caramelized toffee bits and the dip was so cheesy and gooey - what a delicious delight. The egg in both main dishes were cooked to perfection and they are NOT shy with the avocado! Also the matcha vanilla latte is insaneeeeeeee! 

We returned the next morning because, why not?  The way was annoying because it was cold AND windy but worth the wait. This time We started with the banana breas which was exquisite! And I had the tabasco honey biscuit - it was so amazing it was damn near rude! I mean wow! The edges were crisp but dense and doughy and the chicken was insane. I added and egg but it certainly didnt need it! My wife had the bbq toast which she enjoyed but I thought it was just ok in comparison to the biscuit! The absolute star however was the sausage gravy! I have only tried sausage gravy twice previously and both have been disappointments but this?!?! Was absolutely mind blowing Lee delicious. It was chunky but somehow smoothand bursting with flavor with a lovely kick of spice - I mean absolutely insane. I also had a strawberry bellini which was surprisingly delish and of course another matcha latte. 

This is a special spot from the warmth of the folks working there to the lovingly made food. Next time Im in Nawlins, I will make a beeline straight for WIlla Jean without hesitation!
label: positive

text:

text:
Had dinner here last night. If you are looking for underwhelming Italian food at a reasonable price, then this is the place for you.  I started out with Lobster Bisque soup which I thought was way too thick.  I'm an East Coast transplant so I know my seafood dishes.  Lobster Bisque is meant to be more of a delicate soup with a lot of rich flavor.  This soup just did not cut it.  My next course was the Caprese Salad. The tomatoes were underripe and had no flavor.  If you can't get flavorful tomatoes to use  in a tomato salad, you shouldn't serve this salad at all.  Using heirloom tomatoes would have greatly improved the taste of this salad; instead, all the flavor came from their balsamic glaze and the basil which was the topping on the tomatoes and the cheese.  My next course was the Pork Scallopine with mushrooms and a balsamic creamy sauce.  What a surprise when it arrived as two large chewy pork chops which were pounded not so thin.  The typical Pork Scallopine or the Pork Scallopine I have always had in an Italian Restaurant, and how I prepare it at home,  has been made with the tenderloin of pork which is pounded very thin to create a tender, melt in your mouth feel from the pork.  If there are going to serve you a somewhat pounded pork chop, there need to describe it as such.  I asked the owner's wife what cut of pork they used and she told me it was not a pork chop and the server asked the kitchen and she told me it was a pork chop???

I had to give them 2 stars for the service we received and the fact that they exchanged my meal for something else.  The replacement was the Lamb Lollipops which were overcooked and strangely served with waffle fries.  They seem to be a bit confused about what they are serving as Italian food--  waffle fries did not seem to compliment the lamb at all.

I was so hopeful when we found out another Italian Restaurant opened in town since we seem to lack in number in Italian Restaurants compared to the other ethnic ones we have here in Tucson.

I am sorry to say we will not be returning for another disappointing dinner here.
label: negative

text:
I was born and raised in Hong Kong and my parent are from China so I get to know many fish from both side with their real taste for many many years before I moved to American. I love Dim Sum 點心, When I was in Hong Kong I go yum cha 飲茶 for dim sum with my family every Sunday as tradition. I usually go to Philly Chinatown to get real Chinese food, I was excited to see a dim sum place opened up close to where I live and had to try it. First of all there is not a dim sum variety having the restaurant name as dim sum court, it should focus more on dim sum as it main course before any other entrees, I recommend expending more, my favorite dim sum dish like chicken feet and sticky rice with lotus leaf is missing, hope they add it one day. Second I think the owner maybe from China as I can tell from the menu are based in China as it follow the cuisine from China and Shanghai. Anyway enough talking, today I order a few dish to try. Steam Pork Bun 菜肉包, BBQ Pork Bun 叉燒包, Pan Fried Pork Bun 生煎包, Yang Chow Fried Rice 揚州炒飯, and General Tso Chicken 左宗棠雞. 
1. Steam pork bun is on point, the look, size, and taste is good. I recommend this bun, but I don't think it should cost 2.5 a piece, maybe it's inflation.
2. BBQ Pork Bun is also on point, but I think this is a mini version because the regular one is usually bigger. The pork is lean and without fat which I like, sauce and taste is good, the bun itself is soft and good. I recommend this.
3. Pan Fried Pork Bun, this is a Shanghai dish, what I received could be bigger, usually it is bigger size in Shanghai. The taste and look is good, I recommend this.
4. Yang Chow Fried Rice, this dish is missing the main ingredient BBQ pork (Char Siu 叉燒), instead it used shredded pork which is ok if you don't know, to me this is more like the American version of house special fried rice, also it's missing green pea. It need more taste, it's missing its fragrance. I probably won't get it again.
5. General Tso Chicken, no doubt this is an American dish and I don't care much for it, but my wife is American and she love this dish so she got it, the chicken just a little smaller than most Chinese restaurants, but it looks ok and my wife say it's good and it's spicy. I don't think any Chinese restaurants can mess it up but I recently got one from a dim sum place up in this area and it's awful. 
Overall I can recommend this place and will continue to try different dish like soup dumplings and beef chow fun. The price is ok because you can't find a place that serve dim sum up in this area if you are craving them. My order is for takeout and not dine in, so I don't know how the service is.
label: positive

text:
Cafe Du Monde is a must-visit when traveling to NOLA and you definitely cannot miss it given the constant line pretty much during all times of the day outside of the shop. 

We got a bag of the beignets as well as a cup of cafe au lait. Service was fast and efficient and the beignets were all packaged in bags already by the cashier. The bags all come with three beignets, plenty for one person. The beignets were super fresh, crispy, and hot when we got them, and despite the mountain of powdered sugar, had just the right amount of sweetness. It was the perfect pairing with the cafe au lait (we even bought a can of the ground coffee to bring back home)! 

I really loved how simple the business was, to this day, they still only serve beignets and a few drinks (less than 10 items on their menu). Beignets are no-frills, but satisfying, definitely stop by and give it a try! Next time I visit NOLA, Cafe Du Monde will definitely be my go-to breakfast place. 

Tips and Notes: 
1) There is a long line for dining in, but there is a much much shorter to-go line on the other side of the cafe. There are plenty of park benches on the riverwalk not too far from the cafe to take and enjoy your food at. 

2) Cafe Du Monde is cash only. 

3) For those concerned about dining in and COVID, the eating area is outdoors and covered by an awning. The tables are decently spaced out.
label: positive

text:
So there is a reason why this place has really high reviews. Their teas are really good. To add to this, the staff is so extremely friendly. 

Annie Tea House is kind of in an odd shopping center. They are sandwiched between a car/auto parts shop and a medium/high-end furniture store with a pizza restaurant at the end of the plaza. Also, if you drive too fast and miss one of the entrances you have to make 2 U-turns to get back on track on Highway 60. 

I came here after Yelp-stalking my Yelp friend Karen V's reviews. She kind of knows all the cool places and new places to eat in the area. So follow her...just sayin'. 

Anyway, I think I'm a regular customer now because the boba place I used to go to, Quickly Boba Tea, hasn't been walk-in for quite some time and they also don't have the avocado smoothie anymore. So I decided to try a new place and I'm glad I did. Don't get me wrong, Quickly Boba's teas are also spot on, but they may just be losing me in the tug-o-war to find the greatest boba tea place of all time! In the words of the oldest Sunday comic on the planet, Cathy...Ack!

I have tried the taro smoothie with boba, taro coconut smoothie with boba, avocado smoothie with coconut jellies, matcha smoothie with matcha boba, avocado smoothie with boba, avocado smooth with boba and coconut jellies, warm ginger tea latte, iced ginger tea latte, pina colada smoothie with coconut jellies, passionfruit tea and more. I've also tried some of their hot items for a snack to go with my tea and everything is sooo good. My fav cold drinks are the avocado smoothie and the pina colada smoothie. My fav hot drink is the ginger tea latte. The smoothies are thick and not runny which I like because cold things don't stay cold, solid, or semi-solid long in the Florida heat. I can actually take my time sipping on the smoothies, although I don't since they're so good. Then it's all gone and I'm sad. I do think they keep their boba warmed so they don't get all hard like some places have. So you can actually chew them without breaking your teeth. I also think tapioca is gluten-free but don't quote me on that. 

The place is decorated kind of cute so you can hang out and lounge if you want or bring your computer and do some work. 

One note to the world is that if they are busy busy then sometimes it takes 20-30 minutes for the food to be completed because I think they only have one person making food. 

I kind of wish they had a bit more exposure like being able to be found on Grubhub or Ubereats or something so that people further away can experience their teas and food, but maybe they like it just the way it is. So if you're looking for a cool place to relax and want to try a variety of teas, please check out this small business. I think you'll be pleasantly surprised.
label: positive

All of the food was great and I even had a breakfast margarita! I was having a difficult time choosing an Eggs Benedict when I noticed the Peacemaker. You could get one each of two different benedicts, so I couldn't pass that up.

There is a lot of good food in New Orleans, but I can see why this is on everyone's list of must eat at places

text:
Atmosphere: Wow, much nicer inside than I expected. The interior is very clean and classy. Plenty of seating and lots of space. Quiet enough to have conversations and this would be a great place to take a group of friends.  

Food: We have been looking for a Ramen place for a while and so far this is the only one in the area that we know of. The Ramen was excellent, the broth was flavorful, the pork cuts were great and the eggs were also good. I am not a fan of soft boiled eggs but their soft boiled eggs were cooked a little more firm which I appreciated. We frequent this place regularly now. 

Service: Great service. You order from the counter and then someone brings out your food. Most people leave there dishes at the table but  some walk them over the trash area where they have spot for you to leave them. 

Overall: Again, not many Ramen spots in the area but this one is certainly worth your time. We are regulars here.
label: positive

text: I don't have high expectation but my gf was hungry at the time. I got the korean BBQ wings and my gf got the Bun Bo Hue. We also ordered drinks. I have high standards when it comes to BBH. That said... its no where close to being to being good. Not even if its catered towards people who don't know that traditional taste. Korean wings were awful. They are just overly breaded wings inconsistantly drizzled in some type of korean bbq sauce. I already had hesitations since this place is known more for the drinks and desserts more than food. What really turned me off was the service and the atmosphere. I understand this place is new and they are probably understaffed but a lot of things can be changed. Very loud... blaring TV with screaming children is typical for a place like this, but its the also the very loud blenders that make it hard to have any conversations. The owners need to understand that if its a small restaurant than please contain the noise levels. Finally, the unprofessional rude attitude. We had to order at the front desk, pay, take a number and sit. We ate what we can.... and sat and waited without any drinks that we ordered. They gave us a number to bring our food, but they DO NOT bring the drinks. You have to get the drinks yourselves at the front desk when they are done making it!!! We sat their not knowing this "policy" and when we finally asked about our drinks. They told us that it was done. Sitting at the front for us to get. Not only that... its been so long that my mango shush is more like a mango juice. Lol. I have never ate anywhere, even a self service atmosphere, where your food was brought out without any drinks. And when I asked, they said it was our job to monitor our drinks. To get it ourselves... I appreciate that they used actual fruit and not powder to make drinks, but overall the experience was terrible. For anyone thinking about going here, order drinks to go and don't bother getting anything to eat. You might just be doing them a favor.
label: negative

text:
La creperia cafe is one of those hole-in-the-wall places that people go to. When I talk about this place, there's no big hype, but it's like everyone just knows that they have amazing food. It's one of those stable businesses that is enjoyed by locals and tourists alike.

It is located on a busy-ish strip in Ybor, though not too busy during their operating hours. Their crepes are something to look forward to, and you can't go wrong ordering savory or sweet. Pro tip: if you go with a friend, split a savory crepe then split a dessert crepe. Also, get a black coffee to go with the sweet crepes, and get blown away!

The server that we had the last time we were there was very nice and accommodating, and service was quick, too. This was 1pm on a Friday. We people watched from the corner table, and the ambience was just so peaceful that I wish I could just go there everyday and make it my brunch spot. 

5 stars are well-deserved. Will be back.
label: positive

text: So many diverse and delicious options for food, groceries, and dessert. Definitely walk around and see what's all available before making a choice even though you really can't go wrong with much!
label: positive

text:
There was a significant wait and only 1 customer ahead of us, I'm not sure why. We both had the worst Cortado we have ever tasted! We order muffins, in the display was a small muffin that was made in a bakery, it looked delicious.  My mouth was watering like Pavlov's dog. We ordered two of these, what came out was a large muffin that looked like it was a Costco muffin that would have been ok because they have good muffins. Not what I thought we were buying a fresh baked muffin. What we received was a really cheap, sugarless, bland muffin that was made in mass factory quantities. They did warm it up however it must have been frozen because the middle was Icey. It also tasted stale. 

Oh hell no we're never going back $20 for two horrendous Cortados and cheap, stale, partially frozen muffins!

Stay away!!!
label: negative

text:

Even more remarkable, it's a nonprofit organization that helps people in need with their profits. 

I'm finishing this review in my drafts - we visited in 2021. I still miss that amazing chai.

text: I live in the area and have gone to this McDonald's for years. I love McDonald's so I keep coming back but if you go you should expect your order to be messed up. text: Beautiful outdoor patio at this location but beware they close at 1pm. text:
Got the Sunrise Sammy and Eggspanola which are pretty much the same thing with different bread and styles of egg. I personally liked the Sunrise Sammy.

Ordered off DoorDash and I was surprised because for some reason, food that I DoorDash tends to be of lesser quality in comparison to dining in somewhere or picking up the food myself. This meal tasted like it just left the kitchen. Even after letting the sandwich sit for some time (because I got full), it still tasted amazing. I've never thought of sticking a hash brown into a sandwich but there's nothing to hate about it. I'd come here just for the breakfast sandwiches.

text:
I stopped in to grab some Pho after reading some great things about this new business, and I am glad I did. I ordered shrimp Pho with some tofu to go, and the bag had what I needed in terms of condiments. Service was friendly and my order was out lightning fast. I felt like the bag was kind of small and thought I'd be disappointed but that was not the case. Everything was packed tightly, and the portions were quite generous.  The broth was still piping hot when I got home and when all of the ingredients were in the bowl it was a pretty big serving. There were 6 large shrimp (two biters) and 8-10 squares of tofu along with plenty of rice noodles. Also included was a packet that contained bean sprouts, cilantro, basil, and jalapeno slices. Everything was fresh. The broth was flavorful and delicious. 

As a celiac, I won't be trying much of anything else, but I will come back for the Pho!
label: positive

text: Was recommended this place by a local. I scanned their menu by the pictures here on Yelp and decided to try the carrot cake cupcakes. The design of the cake definitely caught my attention. Overall the cupcake was alright it was just more on the drier side. I also tried their strawberry shortcake that a friend of mine had ordered. That one tasted pretty good.
label: positive

text: Love the decor and atmosphere. Everyone is so friendly and the food and drinks are great. This is one of my favorite places to dine.
label: positive

text:
My absolute favorite brunch spot in Philly! There's really nothing really quite like it. I've been here about ~3-4 times now and get something different every single time - I've never been disappointed! 

The dishes I've enjoyed and highly recommend: 
Croque Maude: Combination of all the ingredients I love! On the heavier side - love how thick the bread and how it pairs with the runny yolk. 
Parisian Za: The pizza croissant is actually SO good! The goat cheese and truffle oil top it off super well. 
Fromage Omelet: So beautiful and so tasty! 

Will definitely go back to try more dishes! Truly lives up to the hype.
label: positive

text:
Indoor seating and outdoor patio available.   

When we arrived on this Sunday morning around 10am, we were seated immediately (inside).  I enjoyed the chorizo and chile omelet (which could have been ordered as a fritatta).  I had potatoes and tortillas with my order.  The coffee is delicious and they leave the carafe for coffee and water on your table.  My little enjoyed the Belgian waffle which came with compote and butter.  Added bacon and potatoes.  Sarah our server placed the order as the Trifecta for pricing purposes.  My little gobbled it up.

There are tables, half booths, and booths. 

From some of my previous visits:  The Benedicts are super yummy.  I am not a fan of the million dollar bacon, because I prefer crisp bacon.  I ordered it previously and when my server saw that I did not eat more than the first taste, they offered me something else instead.

Come a little earlier on the weekends; apparently everyone sleeps in:  There were people waiting when we left.  They gave their name and number to the host(ess) and were notified by text message when their table was ready.

Looking forward to trying the Purple Haze (a lemonade with lavender and more).
label: positive

text: Terrible. We ordered chicken shwarma and beef shwarma platters, and they bore no resemblance to shwarma. The meat was just boiled and dry, with no  seasonings and clearly not roasted.
label: negative

text: I have been seeing this place all over Facebook and finally headed over to Clearwater to check it out. I was blown away. So many options on their menu I had a hard time picking something! I went with the lobster and brie omelette and fell in love. Also if it's your first time they give you complimentary beignets and I loved that touch! They came with orange marmalade and I felt like I had dessert before breakfast. This place is only open till two so I can see it getting packed on weekends. But it is in a shopping plaza so there is plenty of parking available

text:
The Mud House is doing fine without my review, but I'll throw my two cents in nonetheless:

It is rare to find a place that balances quality coffee with quality food this well. Their food menu is healthy-ish, quite hearty, and I have enjoyed everything I've tried. Their biscuits and gravy, breakfast burrito, and grilled cheese are standouts.

They now have their own coffee beans, but they also feature local roasters such as Blueprint Coffee. Their syrups are house made--the rosemary syrup in a cappuccino is excellent. While there is better coffee in the neighborhood (Sump is only a few blocks away), there is no better place to grab breakfast or lunch and. Good coffee.

They are frequently busy, but I have always found that by the time I finish waiting in line to order, a table has opened up. Takeout is also a good option.
label: positive

text:
Since having my first experience with the Columbia restaurant in Ybor City, I couldn't pass up having breakfast yesterday on my way back to San Antonio. Well, my experience wasn't 5 stars. I counted about 4 tables seated and 4 waitresses. It wasn't full or anything but, but the wait was ridiculous and I thought maybe I'm suppose to seat myself. I asked the waitress behind the bar and she said someone will get to me. I waited for 15 minutes before being seated. After which, the waitress just wasn't in a mood. 

Now for the food, we'll the prices are way too high for the quality. Also, at 7 in the morning how are they out of Guava pastries? More surprising is the menu I received was very short and nothing like the menus online for this location in Airside E Delta. Coffee was decent and the Cuban bread was ok. I was expecting melt in your mouth rich buttery deliciousness but it was average bread with so-so butter one could get at Publix. I watched others who waited for 10 to 15 minutes for a table while two waitresses argued about an order a current diner stating he never received an item but had actually ate it. 

Sadly, the reputation fell short due to the service times, quality & availability, and prices.
label: neutral

text:
Order: Oat Milk Dirt Rooibos Chai Latte | Highlander Grogg Whole Bean (or Ethiopian Single Origin / Birds & the Bees - I love most of their roasts!!)| Highlander Grogg Sweatshirt Swag | Socks Swag | Nomadic Tea.... the list goes onnnnn!!!!

I love Kaldi's so much! They are masterful roasters with a great selection so you can keep yourself on your toes by bouncing between their single origins & their roasts with super fun names (Natural High, Birds & the Bees, etc). They have shops scattered around STL, including inside the new Fresh Thyme in the Foundary, which I've found to be quite convenient! 

I love that they've teamed up with Nomadic Tea so you can get super special tea lattes like the Rooibos Chai Tea Latte. Such luxury! 

This location is in a really cute neighborhood boutiquey area with outdoor seating. Nice place for a cup of something pretty & a stroll :)

text:
My friends and I found this place on Tiktoc, and let me tell you, it was worth the trip! We were in town celebrating a birthday and decided to give Green Eggs a try. Upon arrival, we were greeted and sat immediately. We started with a carafe of Peach Bellini, which was great. We ordered The Oreo French Toast, Apple Pie French toast and Pecan French toast, along with Shrimp and Grits. Now, I had to send the shrimp and grits back, because they weren't hot. They also only put five shrimp in the entire dish, and I expected a few more. After sending the shrimp and grits back, I decided to order the Red Velvet pancakes, which took a long time to come because they are made to order. By the time they came, my friends were already finished eating! The Red Velvet Pancakes were hot and huge, but definitely lacked flavor-- like it tasted like some plain batter, that's it! 

Let's get into the French Toast... The French toast was absolutely AMAZING!!! Each one we ordered was packed with flavor! The Oreo was my absolute favorite! It was made with Challah bread, and had Oreo stuffed inside. Talk about a foodgasim!!! If you come here, you MUST try the FRENCH TOAST! It's probably legit the best French toast I've had. 

In addition to the food, the service was great as well. The manager was very nice and understanding and took care of the issues we had. If and when I'm back in the area again, I will definitely be back!
label: positive

text: We live in Wesley Chapel and were thrilled when we saw Provisions pop up in the Krates! I had a latte with lavender and my daughter had an iced caramel latte. Both delicious! text:

About $5 for Thai tea.

Was not asked about what heat level we wanted for curry but  Mr Bland ate the curry we received slowly and drank his water. In fact, every table asked for a refill of water.

Also got the green papaya salad that came with about 4 shrimps, too!

text: Why this place doesn't have a solid 6 stars is beyond me. Best boba I've ever had, hands down. text: Wow! What an amazing bakery. Absolutely incredible food and a beautiful atmosphere. Delicious breakfast, the croque madam and breakfast croissant are great but look out for specials, the duck hash is one of the tastiest things I've ever eaten. Pastries are easily the best in Santa Barbara, from simple croissants to decadent and elaborate cakes, the signature candy bar and the pistachio cream croissant have been the most memorable. Seating is a challenge on the weekends so be prepared to wait if you come around breakfast time but once you get one of their seasonal mimosas or a champagne spritz you'll be enjoying yourself before you know it. Still need to try the high tea!
label: positive

text:
I'm from outside New Orleans, but recently moved to Oakland, CA. I frequently Hey! On a monthly basis, as I love specialty coffee and this shop is very consistent and in a few great locations in the city.

My friend Devin, who is a local roaster in Hammond, LA and I had been talking about Yemeni coffee and trying to get our hands on some. For those unfamiliar, Yemen was one do the first places coffee was grown and cultivated. It's also a hard region to export from, modern-day. I was very pleasantly surprised when this showed up at my door, sent from Devin!

I brewed a cup immediately using my Hario V60, and it was fresh and bright. The jasmine came out for me in the tasting notes, giving it a light and almost tea-like body. 

I've always loved this space to frequent, but this was an extra special surprise!
label: positive

text: Whoa mai gawd this place is soooooo good. It was packed on a Monday morning for good reason believe me! Located along an up and coming industrial artsy street, this brunch spot will blow your mind. They have lots of GF options and the food is executed well. I had a GF biscuit (yes a big buttery fluffy buttermilk biscuit) breakfast sandwich with... get this... tomatillo  salsa  marmalade . I am not a sweets person but bacon, cheddar, avocado, rich yummy was complimented with the not super sweet salsa marmalade. The Bloody Mary was effing perf and no woscestershire sauce so def GF. YESSSSSSSSS

text: Hands down - best latte I've ever had. This is for the conscious coffee drinkers who don't mind spending more to support an ethically driven local business. text:
This place is great! I love the pink and green interior and the stone corner cashier stand. The old tile and tin ceilings are also gorgeous. I believe some elements of the interior are left from the space previously being a jewelry store - how cool! I didn't see any old pictures of the store but if the owners have any it could be a great way to decorate. I love the corner it sits on in Fountain Square, right across from the "you are beautiful" sign.

There is window bar seating, tall tables, wall/booth and shorter tables in the room in the back. Also outdoor options. Unfortunately it was a little crowded when I went (around 1 PM on a Sunday, so no surprise) and I got very lucky to be able to find a seat and a parking space in the back. The back room could potentially benefit from a larger table or more smaller tables in the center, but I can see how this might be intentionally left clear for functional purposes. I did notice that the bathrooms are very clean! I love the flamingo wallpaper.

My almond milk latte was good! It came in a paper disposable cup, which had a cute design, but I'd prefer a "for here" or reusable cup option for sustainability purposes. They sell cute merchandise up front, I'd like to get one of the blue travel mugs next time I go. They also sell pastries, though a fresh option like apple or banana would be nice, too. Struggled deciding between 4 and 5 stars, if I could do 4.5 I would. Pleased with this shop, would go here more for socializing than getting a bunch of work done. Glad I finally got to try!
label: positive

text:
I went to Heartbreaker on a recommendation from a friend. I think the name should be wallet-breaker! Don't get me wrong the food was very good but for the money I feel a little filched. The "Breakup Burger" was no more than a double slider plus. I thought that the job that was done with the fries was good! They're very crispy and salted to perfection. I also enjoyed the Chocolate Shake for $6.00! It was 12oz of chocolate heaven!
In total. The patties on the burger were homemade but really the only thing that was different about this burger was the spicy mustard. Fries are fries and the shake was extra chocolatey.

I spent $50.00 for two lunches! In retrospect I think I over tipped. You know how all that's reported in the news is how the wait staff is struggling at restaurants. I thought I would be nice and give a good tip. $10.80 on a bill of $39.20. I would expect to at least be asked how I was enjoying the food, NOTHING. I tipped before I received my food because it's counter service. The staff was more focused on getting out to the picnic table to smoke.

I'm really mixed on how I feel. I feel taken for granted and a$$ fucked at the same time! 

I recommend Heartbreaker just take plenty of money and low expectations with regard to portions and service.
label: positive

text:
Had to stop at this staple! 

Was planning on going here way before our trip. After learning the actual history of this cafe made us want to go even more as it's more than just a cafe. 

It was started by a slave named Rose Nicaud. She purchased her freedom by selling coffee and beignets on Sundays on a push cart in the early 1800s. She saved the portion she was allowed to keep until she had enough to buy her freedom. 

The beignets were pillowy goodness!! There isn't any sugar in the dough itself...all the sugar is the powdered sugar on top. Flavor is delish and fresh. 

The coffee is delish as well. It's made with chicory which gives it its unique taste. 

It is cash only. Yelp states they take cards, but that isn't the case. 

There is plenty of seating but I suggest getting there early as they get crowded quickly.
label: positive

Really enjoyed the iced mocha latte and it's become my go-to at Palo de Azúcar. I also recommend trying some of the baked goods. The pastelito dominicano with chicken was so good and filling!

text:
The last month or two of visits I've notice a bit of a change in the consistency of my drinks. On a positive note, I'm finally getting a jasmine milk tea without the green tea topper. Some days it was green, sometimes it was just the plain ol milk tea you'd expect.  The reason for my update is that I order a large and spend $7 with tip, but what I'm getting is a barely topped off tea with a lot of ice and regulating levels of sweetness. They're shaking their drinks to mix and putting a bunch of ice and showing that it's topped off because the foam is almost to the top but that's a bit shady cause you're not actually getting any tea and I see that there is tea left every time in the shaker. 

$7 dollars for boba in Reno is expensive in my opinion and I'm not even getting organic tea. If you're in the Bay Area with the vast options, this is overpriced even by the standards there. 

I'm hope this gets fixed and I start seeing value to continue my business here and not take it to the other boba shops in town.

text:
what a gem!

This burrito is going to be my go to moving forward! The burrito was flavorful and the meat was cooked perfectly. I appreciated all the sauces and the cheese sides because I love to layer all of them on after each bite. The rice itself is also so good! 

My husband got the fish taco, shrimp and al pastor and he said all of them were tasty but al pastor was his favorite.

All in all this was a great delivery experience and I can't wait to try more things on the menu!

text:
Updating my review within the same day, because the Sabrina's UCity team did contact me today to refund me my money, and also offered for me to come in again soon, so they can set things right. Thank you John M for reaching out and staying true to your word.  I can appreciate when a business seeks to resolve issues in a timely manner.

---

Pains me to write this, because I am a lover of Sabrina's through and through. I've been to multiple locations and I generally love their food. Unfortunately, this is more of a customer support issue, and I am not sure who the blame lies with more: Toast, which is the app they use for Online Ordering, or Sabrina's. Since the Toast app literally has no customer service feature and and is the absolute worst, I'm inclined to place the blame there, but I don't really think Sabrina's was helpful.

Long story short, in my haste I ordered breakfast from this location when I wanted to put the order in at the Wynnewood Sabrina's. Seems like something that should be remedied through the Toast app, right? Wrong. There was no way to cancel the order. So I resigned myself to calling this Sabrina's location and having them cancel the order manually. Bright and early I called and was told they couldn't even find the order in the system. So now I'm side-eying Toast. At this point the host remarked that he wasn't even sure how I made an after-hours order. Well I did. And if that's an issue for the restaurant to control, they need to not even offer evening orders for the next day. Anyway, since they couldn't find my order in the system, he stated I should be OK to re-order at Wynnewood. That wasn't really a satisfying answer for me, since my checking account showed a pending charge for the Sabrina's UCity order. Therefore, I was hesitant to be double-charged both at this location and the Wynnewood location. The next host I spoke to allegedly found my order, but said she wasn't sure how to cancel orders. Ugh. So, I gave her some time, and I called back (mind you she said she would call me, but I couldn't trust that). This time I was told my order was finally voided. OK, good.

I order for pick-up at the Wynnewood location. Easy-peasy. Great service and the food was terrific. A day later I find out I was charged for my food at this location! What was the whole confirmation that my order was voided? I was under the impression that I would be refunded. At this point, I'm not even sure what the restaurant is going to do, and I guess I'm just out almost $50. I hardly know what to say, except that Toast is terrible and the Sabrina's employees would do best to learn their online ordering system and how to rectify orders properly.U
label: positive

text:
Keto or paleo diet? Not the place unless going majoooor cheat style. 

Super cute setting - tight spaced and packed NYC style where you are basically 2 inches from the table next to you and eating with strangers.  Food was amazing - I'll get to that after grievances.

Hate to bring it up since service is struggling every where but service was struggling big time.  We were pressured to order our meal not even 1 minute into sitting down. Friend pointed out we had just sat down and needed some time. And again not even 2 minutes later right on top us to order. Ordered Coffee - wasn't asked if we needed anything with it & it was brought without silverware, spoons, or any sugar option - which turns out they only have ONE sugar option of raw sugar, and it seemed quiet a shock we would ask if there were other options or if we could have a spoon for the coffee...so much that we were brought one spoon for two people for two coffees when we pointed out no one brought us a utensils set up.  It was really bizarre response.  And for a brunch place?

Food itself was absolutely stunning and well crafted, and I'm giving this an extra star completely on the food.  That said - Side of bacon was not- cold and not cooked enough, but banana bread, shaksuka, etc was all really impressive.  Seems this place has a hard time with the little things, but main menu items are really great.
label: positive

text: I am super disappointed. I love the Jeannine's in Montecito. But this one is just not that great. text:

text:
Service was good but the food was okay. We started off with Crispy Green Beans and Chicken Bites, both were delicious. The green beans had the perfect crunch and a delicious sauce with a little spice. The bites were very crunchy which I loved and you can pick from three different sauces.

The pho was a let down considering the name of the restaurant is Mopho. I love pho and eat it frequently but the pho here tasted more like ramen. I got the beef pho but I think the mushrooms and egg in it made it less like pho. That didn't bother me, but the broth was very bland and that's usually the best part. 

We shared a blondie for dessert and didn't even finish it. There were too many flavors going on with this dessert that just didn't work. It had a miso caramel, blondie, toasted apples on top with a sesame whipped cream. It was an interesting combination but it didn't work. 

The appetizers were great, but everything else was okay.

text:

They also brought a dog bowl for my fur baby. Service was quick and easy, a crucial for lunch. Definitely will come back if I return to Saint Pete's.

text:
I was looking for something lite and sweet and came across this establishment. 
I was able to find street parking directly in front of the business.  I don't think finding street parking will be hard as there is typically space.  
We were greeted by a young man at the register who showed us the menu behind him and in the counter.  Everything was so colorful and there were so many options I couldn't choose. Selecting a crepe was the easy part because I had no clue what some things were on the menu for the dessert bowls.  The young man behind the register was very patient with me as I had a few questions about some of the ingredients since I hadn't heard of them before.  Boy, he was so amazing!!! Whatever it was that I ordered was great. It had the mango slushee which was sweet and tasted like mango puréed juice, mangos, strawberries and other fruits (I feel like I tasted grapes that had been chopped up but whatever they were they were sweet and good). There were also small boba balls (not sure of exact name) but everything about this dessert was amazing.

text:

text:
I've been on hunt for a great study/work spot to hang out in when I need a change of scenery and I got just that. The atmosphere is so inviting and cozy here. There is plenty of seating so you don't have to worry about popping in for work and not being able to find a seat. The noise level was also perfect. I was a little worried that because this is a bigger coffee shop and it's downtown, it may be loud but it really wasn't. 

I ordered a cafe con leche and a Cuban sandwich and everything tasted delicious, flavorful and fresh. They do make the cafe con leche with condensed milk instead of regular milk which resulted in a foamier coffee. However the Cuban sandwich was made to perfection. I can tell it was fresh because the cheese melts away with each bite and each component of the sandwich was crispy and hot just the way I love it! 

All in all, if you're looking for a chill study or workspace, I'd encourage you to stop by. I can't wait to come back!
label: positive

text: Love this place and the idea behind it! The owners are so friendly and talkative! I loved it when they told us about the cats and their personalities! The cake pops were absolutely delicious and super cute! The aesthetic of the place was so pretty, even the bathrooms were decorated nicely. Would definitely recommend if you want to relax with some cute kitties! (:
label: positive

text:
I came in with a group of 8 on a Wednesday night and we were sat right away in the bar area. It looked as if they had a lot of support staff which is good bc a lot of places have been struggling with help.
We were helped right away and our drinks and apps came out soon after. Our food came out in a timely manner and everyone had nothing but good things to say about their entrees. Our waitress was pleasant and helpful. Overall we had a good experience and would be open to coming back next time I am in town.
4/5
label: positive

text: Perfect breakfast place! Great coffee and food in a super cute location. Everything bagel, yogurt, and lavender honey latte were perfect.
label: positive

text: Love this little bit of Paris on the streets of Tampa. Food we had was great both the Strasbourg crepes and the Parisienne baguette sandwich. The sidewalk tables mimic a Paris cafe although it's not so much people watching as "car watching" given they look at the street!
label: positive

text: I've been trying to make my way here since I first saw the king cake beignet on Instagram. I'm happy this place did not disappoint. It was very busy and we barely found a seat. Please keep in mind there is no wait staff. You have to order at the bar. I had the panini which came with truffle fries. The prosciutto had a strong taste which I personally did not care for but if you like prosciutto I'm sure this sandwich would work you. The truffle fries were delicious. I ate them all and they were cold! The king cake beignet had the traditional beignet dough flavor with a sweet glaze. The filling tasted of cream cheese and walnuts. I tried the queso and was surprised it tasted so freshly made. The flatbread was amazing. I'll get it next time I go. I also want to try the beignet flight. This place would make for a cute date.
label: positive

text: Highly recommend this great place for dessert or breakfast. The chai tea was excellent. Macarons were expertly made. The pastries were so fresh and tasty. We found this place by accident and so glad we did!
label: positive

text:
Not as impressive as I thought. The bagels are good overall but the "everything bagel" has too much salt! 
They have vegan cream cheeses as well, but we tried their harissa and jalapeno cream cheese and both of them were quite disappointing. We were told they were "spicy" but it lacked any sort of kick.
They have cute outdoor sitting so a good cafe/place to work and hang out.
label: neutral

text: Very fresh, delicious and healthy.  The Miso wrap is excellent and I also tried the West Coast.
label: positive

text: Just went thru the drive thru at 3am and the girls there tonight were professional polite and friendly that's something you don't normally see at this hour.  Kudos to those ladies!  ThAnk you
label: positive

text: This is a very friendly place with distinctive fare. The food is fresh and authentic.
label: positive

text: Jamba Juice is great! I always get the Red Gummy but today I decided to try something different and I regret it... I got the Chunky Strawberry and requested 2% Milk instead of Soy Milk. And I asked for blueberries on top as well. When I went in, the employee handed my Bowl. Assuming I paid but I'm a good person to stand there and wait for him to take my payment and he forgot. I could have easily walked away but I didn't.... When I got home I noticed I did not have Blueberries on top of my bowl. And the moment I tasted my Bowl I could tell it was with soy milk and not 2%... immediately that RUINED my entire digestive system and my stomach hurt right away. I'm just a bit disappointed. I guess I should stick with the Red Gummy moving forward :/
label: neutral

text:
Midtown Boba is a fairly new boba place located in the new Midtown area on Dale Mabry. It's kind of easy to miss since it's in the same shop as a spin cycle studio. It's small, there's only about 5 seats but it's still cute and new and trendy.

The staff were very friendly and I got the Oreo boba because it just looks so good. Unfortunately, it just didn't wow me at all. In my opinion,I have had better boba. I could hardly taste any Oreo in my drink and the boba itself was very bland. I think what made me give this a fail was the Oreo issue. It really just tasted like regular milk tea. Not sure if I will return.
label: neutral

text:
I really enjoyed my experience here. You could easily spend the entire day. From coffees to amazing teas, salads to desserts. Everything is beautifully prepared and delicious. 
The tea selection is really quite impressive and you can have it anyway you want it...even as a cocktail. 
I had the seasonal salad and it was fantastic. Fresh and filling. 
The restaurant has indoor and an indoor garden seating area. Both are spacious. Apart from the restaurant there is a gift shop with really exclusive items...from cards, ornaments, dinnerware, jewelry and toys. Also the book store...books are everything! There is also a section in which you can just enjoy coffee, tea and pastries. 
It's a lovely place definitely coming back.
label: positive

text: The idea of the place is very fun but, I would not recommend the food. If you're looking for flavor I would not recommend the rice bowl as it is obsolete.
label: negative

text: Excellent dim sum restaurant in Chinatown, it's like a hidden gem. The flavor and the selection of dim sum resembled the one I ate when I was living in Hong Kong, I can tell it's good. My daughter loves the lotus leaf sticky rice, it's her favorite place to get. I also had many other dish there as they all taste like the standard Hong Kong dim sum taste, I give this place a high mark for taste and selection.
label: positive

text: Awesome food! Beignets we're fresh and hot. Fast service. We came by around 10pm and had no trouble getting food. Definitely one of the must go to in the city!!!!
label: positive

text:
If you're looking for a meal that tastes homemade, Buendía is just your place. Their food tastes authentic and made with love. Nothing tasted processed or overly salty... Just delicious. 

I got a cafe de la olla coffee with a nopal huevos rancheros plate. The cafe de la olla had a very strong cinnamon taste and wonderful smell. With the huevos rancheros, I was expecting the nopal to be chopped up in the eggs or salsa but instead, the eggs were placed on top of a whole nopal leaf. I had never had nopal like this before but it was really tasty!! Having the whole leaf allows you to appreciate the nopal a lot more. 

Overall had a great experience here!
label: positive

text: I wish I had asked the lady who helped us for her name- she was so incredibly friendly & sweet! The blueberry cheese danish was the best I've ever had & my boyfriend also enjoyed his almond croissant. We also got the little pretzel sandwiches for a light lunch and they were good too! Loads of options here & I can't wait to go back for more!
label: positive

text:
I was visiting friends in St. Pete and we decided to hunt down a local shop for some coffee. I am very much a latte lover and always looking to experience new and exciting flavors and trends. When coming to Kahwa just know they have exceptional coffee but most of their choices are pretty straight forward. Don't try and hunt down anything too fancy here :) 
I got a vanilla latte with oat milk and it was smooth and delicious. It was well balanced and I enjoyed it.
label: positive

text: Great coffee spot (probably best in STL).  They offer all types of coffees properly prepared.  Whether you have keto needs or just plain prefer oat milk they've got you covered.  And every coffee is made by a trained no joke serious barista.  And service is always presented with a smile.
label: positive

text:
When we tried Wow Wow Hawaiian Lemonade for the first time, I was transported back to Hawaii and started missing the beach!
There are a lot of options for lemonade flavors and the staff are knowledgeable about flavor profiles and what you might like. We had the blackberry lavender and pina colada, they were both delicious and I would get either again in a heartbeat. 
The price might look high initially, however the ingredients are fresh and well worth it. I will be back soon!
label: positive

text: These bagels are solid. If you need a fix for a New York bagel and you live south of the city, this is the place to go.
label: positive

text: Visited with family for breakfast. We ordered a sharing platter, butter and sugar crepes, nutella crepes and a bacon brioche and everyone was very happy with their meals. The waiter and waitress were very friendly and attentive and made it a pleasant experience for us. Would love to visit again for dinner sometime!
label: positive

text: Last Watch is more like it. This restaurant is so short staffed. We waited about 20 minutes before our drink order was brought to our table. We also waited over an hour for food to come out. The restaurant itself was not too busy but the online/takeout orders were their priority. We must have watched at least 20 take out orders come out of the kitchen. We also did not get our food at the same time. We were a party of 10 (some of us even shared an order) and one dish at a time was brought to us at a time about 5‐7 minutes apart. My daughter was done eating about 20 minutes before I received my order that was missing a soup. I did not see any manager or supervisor on duty. The only reason that it got 2 stars instead of 1 is that the food was pretty decent. We definitely felt like we were on the Last Watch instead of First Watch.
label: negative

text:
This place just wasn't my cup of tea.  
I really like how they had the outdoor seating set up, I was very warm. Warm enough that I could take my jacket off and I was getting hot.  Servers were attentive to all customers in the outdoor seating area.  When I say all customers, I mean all, those on 2 legs and those with 4.  
They allowed dogs inside the outdoor seating area which I had not seen until I ordered my food.  Servers were seen petting dogs and then coming to serve you your food. I do not appreciate having to eat a meal in an establishment and have a dog shaking itself, especially in an enclosed area. 
I wasn't overly impressed with my meal. I ordered the eggs, meat, hash brown and pancake/French toast.
label: neutral

text: Visiting Java Ship has become a staple in my routine. The owner is very friendly, the drinks are fantastic, the prices are good, and there are a lot of options. I love the chia seed pudding and americano, but there are many other sweet drinks and there is almost always a speciality drink. I could not recommend this strongly enough!
label: positive

text:
Alessia came from a restauranteur family and brings decades of experience including time at Bouchon. The pastry counter is open at 7 but breakfast isn't served until 8. It's right across from the El Presido with a clean, classy interior.

Breakfast: We got the crab crepe, french toast, and granola yougurt for the kids. Everything was well-cooked and beautifully plated, especially the yogurt dish. Only feedback is slightly too much hollandaise on the crepes and the french toast was a little soggy on bottom. Otherwise high quality and well portioned. 

We wanted to give the kids some solid food and ordered a croissant, it was so good we got up while waiting for our breakfast and got more pastries for later.

Pastries: 5 stars, the croissant is better than the one at Bouchon and Alexanders. It has an incredible outside crunchy layer and soft cloud-like inside. Cheddar bacon scone and kouign-amann also amazing, loved having these for a quality breakfast the next day.
label: positive

text: AMAZING FOOD!  Everything we tried was fresh, made to order and tasted Wonderful!  Switched entrees with my dad, he didn't see that the gravy was spicy.  I love spicy and thought it was more medium spice level.  I wasn't sad to switch.  The potatoes were my favorite but that is because I love anything potatoes!  Highly recommend!
label: positive

text:
We came here, very pleased, spent a lot of money on amazing treats. Naturally sweetened, not much refined sugars and amazing ingredients. 

My wife and I were harassed by another customer for I had forgot my mask, and she cursed at us, and flicked us off on her way out.  As soon as she left she proceeded to take her mask off 1 foot outside of the store. 
Staff was kind enough to see this and offer me a mask. But this rudeness ruined our appetite and we couldn't really appreciate the good desserts. We hope we can try it again when we return to the city.
label: positive

text: Awesome donuts and coffee! Staff is always friendly , place clean, there may be a line but its so worth the wait!
label: positive

text: The food was very good and the service was perfect. It does get a little busy, but the wait was not bad considering we are in a pandemic.
label: positive

text: Everything that I have tried has been very flavorful! My favorites are the Carne Asada and fish tacos blackened while my husband prefers the Nashty and the Dos Perros. If you need an appetizer go for the Mexican flag so you can try their two salsas and the queso! With delicious ingredients like these you can't possibly make a poor choice!
label: positive

text:
We waited in line as they allowed someone with a huge order ( when they should've called it in!) to take almost everything left so my kids and I were in line waiting almost 20 minutes) were upset. Plus they ordered tons of sandwiches and we would have to wait for a long time to get anything left! My kids watched as the stuff got cleared out! 
They shouldn't allow people to take huge orders like this unless they give notice first! My kids love this place so much ( have been looking forward to this place for breakfast all week and were upset!) and have always been happy with everything! This time we were inconvenienced and had to deal w upset kids.
Tell people for huge orders- they have to call first!
label: negative

text:
I was in the area to shop at Tucson's biggest Asian food store for banh mi ingredients and just so happened to see Banhdicted next door.

Everyone was super nice and the store was adorable inside; they've got some storage overflow in the main building but every taqueria and Asian food place in town does right now (coronavirus pandemic year 3!). 

Since I'm making a classic sandwich I decided to order their lemongrass beef sandwich; great decision. The baguette was fresh and crackled in my hands, the pickled vegetables were crisp and flavorful, and the beef was absolutely delicious! The flavors blended well together and the warm tender beef went perfectly with the pate. 

I plan on coming in the future to try more, and can't wait to check out some of the specials. Thank you!
label: positive

text: The food is awesome here and the server could not have been more efficient and pleasant.  I was there for breakfast with my husband who was equally impressed.  I can't wait to return and try some of their brunch items.  I intend on trying to make a weekly visit.
label: positive

text:
AMAZING WARM BEIGNETS WITH MOUNTAINS OF POWDERED SUGAR IN THE BAG. I went on Sugar Bowl weekend/NYE weekend, so there were a lot of people and long lines. Luckily the lines go by quickly because the beignets are ready at the cash register. Keep in mind they only sell beignets and coffee. You get 3 large pieces of beignets, which are super filling. Highly recommend this spot for all visitors!

Pro tip: there are 3 separate lines, so pick the shortest one! You find a seat after you're gotten your food and drinks at the register
label: positive

text: I feel obligated to write yet another poor review of this restaurant. Our first visit (back in 2019) was good - nice ambiance, great server and good food. BUT since then, every time we say 'let's give them another try' ....just awful service, sub par food and the inside can get very noisy! It's a shame since the inside of this old church is charming...why can't they do a better job? (other places are dealing with 'stuff' too but seem to hold it all together) This place seems to not care!
label: negative

text: These are the folks I call when I have a lot of guests at the last minute.  Their food preparation is first rate.  Quality of food is also first rate.  What makes them over the top is how easy it is.  Just a call to Justen.  Always on time, the food is hot, delicious, and correct to the order.  Never had one of their bagels, yet, however!  We use them all the time for catering.  Keep doing what you're doing!
label: positive

text: We were super hungry and got chicken quesadilla, shrimp tacos, guacamole and beef empanadas. The food tasted oddly bland and stale. Unfortunate yet tolerable. Guacamole was good though and their chips!
label: neutral

text: Such a wonderful place! So peaceful and relaxing athmophere. Nice river view, good beer, bad jokea and good company!
label: positive

text: We looked up TripAdvisor and found this place, and on arrival thought we had the wrong place. Don't be fooled! This is the correct place and definitely deserves the high ratings!!! You can have delicious spaghetti with authentic naan + tandoori chichen or lovely nasi daging, the chef excelled in all cuisines! MUST TRY MUSHROOM SOUP. Food was amazing
label: positive

text: wonderful food and staff. the food is very nice and family friendly. Just make sure u order non spicy for the kids, my kids especially love their chicken mandi rice, and also hummus with bread. their food is fresh and hot, will come back again next visit for sure
label: positive

text: luigi and boro are the best waiters on this earth the bruschetta was pretty good and the drinks were strong !! def will come back when I'm in philly !!! LOVE THIS PLACE
label: positive

text: Great food and such nice people!! Pastries are amazing and the toasted croissant sandwiches are so so good. Tomato pesto mozzarella is my favorite! Definitely recommend!
label: positive

text: What a pleasant experience! I ordered a western omelette which was delicious. Everything was served hot and quickly. The service here was absolutely AMAZING- our server was pleasant and friendly. She did not make us feel rushed at all but she knew when to check in from time to time. I will without a doubt be back and if you are in the area, I would stop in and check this place out.
label: positive

text: My daily is an oat milk honey latte. They make a great one at Osa Coffee Roasters. Nice people...great area and they roast their coffee on-site.
label: positive

text:
I don't understand all the negative reviews. I've been here at least once a week since their opening and they have been nothing but professional with excellent customer service. I wanted to ensure that was clear before I continue. It is possible the negative reviews were one offs and although situational experience is valid I would like to express awe and gratitude at my experiences at Freshii (all six of them) this year. If I could write six reviews and give each review four stars, I would, if only to get their rating a little higher. 

At this point,  I have tried all salads on their menu and they are all great! My favorites are the cobb and the fresh salad. The ceasar can be a little dry but all you have to do is ask for extra dressing and they will kindly provide more. 

My husband is a fan of their soup and pocket combo. The seasonal butternut squash soup is AMAZING, it is a must try if it's available. 

They have a rewards program if you download their app, get points that add up to free items. 

I believe Freshii is a much needed place in our community, healthy take out. We order door dash (because they are available on door dash and uber eats) all the time and they arrive within 30 minutes. 

I grant them four stars because they are a bit expensive and sometimes the food is lukewarm BUT you can ask them to warm it up for you. 

All in all, I am very happy with this place and have brought my mother AND mother in law to eat here (new year new me ,you know #DietTime). They love it as well!
label: positive

text: Not bad. We had the Cuban pollo platter which was very tasty. The chicken breast was small and dry but the flavor- just the right amount and combo of spices - was good. The beans and rice were excellent and the maduros were absolutely addicting. It gets a minus one star for uncomfortable seating inside and out; and dirty tables and floors.
label: positive

text: Joe Tierney explained the issues we were having with our existing system and how Pure Water Systems would correct them.  Three days later our new system was installed by Chris Schuth.  We are very pleased with the system and with our dealings with both of these men. Dayton, Nevada, NOT San Francisco, California.
label: positive

text:
My body feels like the food I ate BAD. $60 spent on a lunch for (2) and we both only enjoyed very little of our meal. We threw the rest away. A classic case of a restaurant with good photography but crappy food. 
Our cheesesteaks were 0.2/10. 
Wings 4/10. 
Milkshake 4/10. 
Fried Oreos 5/10. 
Fries 6/10. 

Little Man's ended up being a BIG disappointment.
label: negative

text: They get my order wrong 90% of the time and they always seem grouchy and irritated that your making them work
label: negative

text:
It's been a long time since we visited, but Corner 17 is always one of our favorite restaurants. We just visited again Dec 2021 with friends. There was a 20 minute wait for a seat at 7pm. They take your phone number and text you when your table is ready. We waited in our car since there were a lot of people waiting at the front door.

We were seated near the window where the chef is making the noodles by hand. He's pulling them to make longer and longer noodles. He's shaving them into the boiling water to make the shaved noodle dishes. He's making the dumplings right in front of us and folding the perfect little purses in just 3 seconds each. BEST SEAT IN THE HOUSE!

We started with the wontons in chili sauce. I was a little worried it would be crazy spicy, but they were perfect and did not light my tongue on fire. I also grabbed a few chicken skewers since they are juicy and covered in a honey sauce. The chicken satay appetizer comes with 3 skewers.

 You can't visit Corner 17 and not get their noodles. We had the Sichuan Spicy Noodles with bok choy and ground pork in a Sichuan spicy sauce. There's extra chili oil on the table if you need even more spice!

I always order the sesame balls with sweet red bean filling. They come out piping hot and squishy. I could eat the whole plate by myself. These are also good when they are room temperature, so grab an order to go.
label: positive

text: Great Bloody Mary "get with the bacon" worth the extra, ohhh and the cheesy grits are amazing!!
label: positive

text:
During my trip to Nashville I was advised to visit this local coffeeshop, and I've already made two trips this week. The frothy monkey seems to be well loved by locals, so it's a little busy but has a very cosy atmosphere. We came on a weekday afternoon so we were seated as soon as we walked in. (Indoor and outdoor options)

I was surprised to see they have such a vast menu, not just coffee and lattes but they also prepare breakfast and brunch foods as well as wines. I tried a French toast there and it was alright.

I LOVED their golden monkey drink. I felt like it was so perfectly balanced. Just the right amount of creaminess, giving it great mouthfeel combined with the right blend of spices. I appreciate that it was not overly sweet like so many other coffee shops' drinks. It was honestly one of the best golden milk latte I've had yet, and I'm someone who's been drinking turmeric milk since my childhood. I can't forget to mention the pretty latte art designs that top each cup
label: positive

text:
I like playing the live cards. Three card poker is my favorite. I had a hand dealt and used a call from the pit boss regarding the cards I was dealt that are used to compete against the dealer with. I also was using the separate odds area on the table and won a $200+ simultaneous deal. The pit boss rejected my plea about my hole deal. He then retreated out of my field of play. The dealer then quickly snatched my winning chips and I was left with nothing.
I then looked over the dealer's shoulder and saw the pit boss snickering as he watched the saga unfold.
This so called casino is regulated by itself and they rip off its patrons at every turn.
label: negative

text: We love the mint fox! Definitely my favorite spot on Clearwater Beach for ice cream and cookies. The owners are so nice and friendly and provide great, customer oriented service. I also love their warm, freshly baked cookies and their numerous ice cream flavors!
label: positive

text: Inside of reading terminal there's this amazing gem! Old city coffee must be the best coffee I've ever had. I had a ice mochachino with caramel and my fiance had a hot chocolate with whipped cream. Both were delicious! I'm a heavy coffee shop coffee drinker so I have been exposed to different coffees in my years. My fiances hot chocolate was very thick and creamy and very chocolaty! Hopefully if I'm ever back in Philly I'll be sure to stop by!
label: positive

text: Two frenchaise will never fails ! The food and the hospitality of the staff is excellent, well trained ! Especially one of the staff name Ikram. Amaazing food! The whole experience from start to finish is great server, is always so friendly and kind. The food can’t get better and the prices are fair for the portion size. Always a great spot to get great food.
label: positive

text:
The food is freshly made, it taste delicious. I had the breakfast burrito with a hot chocolate. 
The ambiance is relaxing, not too busy. There is free parking avail in front and back of the business. 
This place gets an extra star for being dog friendly. You can sit indoor or outdoor with your dog! 
If you are in downtown Reno, def worth checking out.
label: positive

text:
Wow! Hands down, the best Cuban sandwich in Florida. I live in Miami, and have been to many little Cuban bakeries and famous restaraunts and not even one of them came close to this one.
The bread is toasted to perfection, yet soft on the inside, filled with an abundant amount of pork, ham and cheese. I ordered a half size and it left me wanting more. My boyfriend ordered a media noche, wow it was good also. I ordered a guava and cheese pastelito. I have never tasted a better one, it was soft with just enough sweetness but not overwhelming. 
This is a must go place!!!! Don't miss it. They know how to do sandwiches.
label: positive

text: FitLife Foods has been a staple of mine for healthy work food ever since I tried it at my job in St. Anthony's six years ago, and they seem to get better and better the more they expand (although they have closed alot of locations). The menu items do come and go, as they switch up their offerings depending on what's in season, but I always manage to find something I like. Some of the offerings they have dont even sound healthy, but shockingly, they are. I'm talking about their meatloaf (which they improved immensely, and I'm a sucker for their cauliflower mash) bbq beef with mac n cheese, and pumpkin cheesecake. That's right. Pumpkin. Cheesecake. So light, creamy, and rich, it actually manages to RIVAL its unhealthy full-fat counterpart. It provides a nice hearty three forkfulls (cause i may be kinda a pig, but not a slob) and finish great with black coffee. My other fave is their bbq beef mac and cheese. Tender, sweet tasting, hearty pulled bbq beef with burnt edges and creamy mac and cheese fills me up and satisfies me. This dish is so good it has me wondering what the point of a cheat day is if my normal work food is just as good if not at times better???!! They also have fresh breakfast entrees that I like to get for my Sunday "morning brunch" even though im not brunching and im going to work, but im not feeling bloated and weighed down. There's a large selection to choose from there. Love their sweet potato pancakes with bacon the most, along with their french toast and banana oatmeal bake (which is rather unique and original!). The new packaging makes the food last forever, which is a big plus, and you still get a discount for bringing clean packages to recycle. What's NOT to love???
label: positive

text: Delicious and fresh ingredients, terrific and enthusiastic service and fairly reasonable prices. Been here many times and always enjoy the food and service.
label: positive

text: I love a breakfast place with so many healthy options I can't decide! This is a rare gem I'm so delighted continues to pop up everywhere! Try the unique juice options, and that strange entree you're just not sure? Go for it, amazing every time! I'm not a morning person but I'll get up early for this set of options, always epic!
label: positive

text: Quaint. Original recipes, I had the squash omelette... yummy. Service was really slow, but they were swamped (testimony to their popularity) I took home some of their pastries home with me, all of them were delicious.
label: positive

text: Cool, down to earth juice bar in Germantown. It was a crazy cold and rainy day when we attempted to walk around the strip and this was a bright spot. I ordered a spinach smoothie which was tasty and not overly sweet. My group really enjoyed their smoothies too. You can also ordered some food if you're hungry!
label: positive

text:
Great staff. Kiersten and Kathy do it again with a smile behind that mask

As you can see from the mushroom croissant that my friend had they are very generous with the mushrooms. Even though sometimes there a fair amount of people when you when we regularly find an outdoor seat

I bring my own sea salt.  It's particularly good on the mushroom cheese croissant
label: positive

text: Returned to this restaurant to try out other things on their menu. We had the shredded beef empanadas, the pabellón entree (shredded beef, plantains, rice and beans) and the arepa llanera (shredded beef, white cheese, tomato and avocado). The empanadas were delicious but the pabellón and the arepa were really bland, missing salt and seasoning. I'd definitely recommend this place for the cachitos and Venezuelan empanadas, although pricey they're really good. I'd skip the rest of the menu items though.
