text:
I'm not a coffee drinker but they have the best smoothies -and hot chocolate! Especially the seasonal items are the best! 

Highly recommend the strawberry, mango, peach smoothie with whip! 

Each on my experiences have been the best ever! I go probably two times a day ‍
label: positive

text: I'm gonna keep this really short and simple, just like this places menu. The food was spectacular, I mean we're talking about a really good good hamburger. We got the regular heartbreaker and then I got the one with the bacon and bbq sauce on it I think it's called the smoke. It was nothing short of amazing. French fries were perfect. Even the milkshake was perfect! Go now
label: positive

text:
Fantastic location, and the outside dining area is awesome.  The outside tables are not spaced too close together so even in covid times you feel comfortable not being crammed together.  The food menu is limited compared to their regular restaurants if you're expecting that, so just be aware.  In particular, this location does not have paella, particularly their ridiculous Paella "A la Valenciana" that I drive an hour to get at the Ybor location.  It is what it is, this location is intended to be a faster experience, not the multi-hour Ybor type setup.  The mojitos are prepared table-side just like in Ybor, and are as delicious as expected.

This is a really great option if you're staying at the Marriott; short walk, much better food, better prices.  Or, enjoy it before a Lightning game so you aren't eating over priced slop at the arena.
label: positive

text:
Visiting from SF - this place is excellent. Don't be out off by it's location in a mall. 

The food and service are top notch and the decor inside is super cute. 

We had the French toast and Avo toast with matcha lattes made with oat milk. 

Everything was made perfectly and was really delicious. The eggs had deep Orange yolks and were also super tasty. 

Two thumbs up!

text:

text:
Look, I get it. You have brunch fatigue. I have it too. I'm tired of having to pay $45 just to day-drink and eat eggs that are cooked a bit better than I could cook them at home. I understand the feeling of trepidation when someone says "Let's try this new brunch place!" because 8 times out of 10 they will have the same variety of american breakfast that's upcharged due to having a bit of seafood on it and it'll be ok but somehow more expensive than lunch??? 

So I'd get it if you swore off brunch places forever. But before you do... can I recommend you a brunch place? It's called Bearcat. It serves "food coma," "I can't believe I ate so much," "I want this to be my last meal" food. It's the same price as every other brunch place except you won't be annoyed when the bill comes.

Caveat:
It's definitely a pain as they do not take reservations and they're always packed. Today I waited 45mins to be seated at the bar with my two friends which I was not expecting and was a bit disappointed at because I wanted to get a table and be able to look my friends in the eye and hear what they were saying. They really should ask if the bar is ok before seating you there.

All that aside, everything else about my experience was perfect just like last time. The staff was very kind and attentive and the food was incredible. I got the chai pancakes because they are the best pancakes I've ever had and may ever have in my life. I have no idea what you would even have to do to make pancakes this good... get the chai ones!!! The upcharge is worth it!!! I also got the seafood pot pie.... My god.... It was totally full of seafood, not skimpy at all, and the scallops tasted high quality and were cooked to perfection. I'm not sure what the "catch of the day" was that was in there but I scarfed it down. The lobster sauce was delicate, the veggies were perfectly cooked, and the presentation was lovely. My cappuccino was lovely as well. And they serve you your food on the most beautiful custom made plates with hand-thrown pottery cups that have their adorable little cat logo on them...... !!!!!!!!! The attention to detail is so appreciated. I wish I could eat here every day.
label: positive

text:
Cherry Espresso Bar is located inside a building that is more than 100 years old. New Orleans coffee shops are often special places and this is another unique spot to enjoy your coffee and tea. This location next to Wisner Park was previously the old Laurel Street Fire Station and then it later served as a New Orleans Recreation Department Community Center before the city auctioned it off which led to us being able to enjoy some great coffee at Cherry Espresso Bar.   They serve some great coffee, tea and other tasty beverages along with a selection of baked goods.  This is a great spot to enjoy your caffeine and it has a good variety of seating options both inside and outside.  When you find yourself in the mood for some tasty hot beverages you should check this place out.

My video review of Cherry Espresso Bar is here:
https://youtu.be/iV1AQiz7wc8
label: positive

text:
Amazing! I can't wait to return. Our mochas were perfect--not to sweet and smooth.

Our Savory crepe was chicken, spinach,  and bacon with cream sauce. I'm so glad I took a chance on a small French cafe in Tampa. I've been to Montreal and enjoy French cuisine. 

The baguette and brie was so tasty. I send a kiss to the Chef! Thank you for being our highlighted meal of Tampa. I will see you again.

Prices and portions are reasonable.  Actually I think it was unpriced based on the quality of the food. I can't wait to eat there again!

Customer service was mediocre but I don't let that influence my experience. It was a cold and rainy Sunday morning. The host/server was from Poland (didn't get her name) but she wasn't thrilled to see us walk in. We were greeted very underwhelming. I don't internalize bad customer service but I still notice it.

Parking is okay. We arrived at 7:30am so the streets were clear. I know how crazy it gets Downtown so I anticipate walking a block or 2 on my next visit.
label: positive

text: Was looking for a new place to try and stopped in. Love the menu! Not the run of the mill breakfast items. Got the French toast with fresh fruit! So delicious! They even warmed the maple syrup! Loved it so much, I took my son when he visited. He loved it too! Definitely worth the visit.

text:
I recently came to French Truck Coffee during a trip to New Orleans.  I actually saw this place on the way to get a slice of cake at Bakery Bar down the street and decided I'd bring the cake here and pair it with some coffee.  Upon entry, I liked the place's simple and clean aesthetic.  There were an adequete amount of tables to sit at.  The guy who took my order seemed very nice and friendly as well, which is always a big plus. 

I ended up just getting a small coffee ($2.50).  I think it probably came out to $2.75 after tax.  I think there were 3 types of roasts to choose from: medium, dark, and chicory.  I just got the medium roast but looks like chicory is their specialty so too bad I didn't try that one.  I'm not a coffee expert, but I felt their medium roast was excellent.  It was buttery smooth going down my throat and noticably better than any other chain coffee I've had.  

I think the combination of all things here make it deserving of a 5 star rating.  If I'm ever in New Orleans again I'll drop by to try the chicory.
label: positive

text: Delicious French breakfast, real bread, real croissants, Street side, not pedantic.  The macchiato is an Italian-style macchiato, not the Starbucks version .  Friendly personal service in French or English.  Just awesome.

text:
Stopped at Willa Jean for a cute and quick solo brunch! I grabbed a seat at the bar as the actual dining area was pretty full on a late Monday morning. I opted to start with a frosé which was really good! I ordered my food and it promptly came, I was so excited! I had the Willa Jean breakfast which includes 2 sausage links, 2 strips of bacon, cheese grits, eggs, and a biscuit. The grits and eggs were soooo good! The sausage was good also, but the bacon was a bitttttt too salty for me. Would I recommend this dish? Yes. 

After eating my meal, I couldn't help but feel pressured to indulge in something sweet, as their pastries and bakery items were very visually pleasing. I originally ordered banana pudding which was not good at all. The pudding part was greatly lacking flavor. Luckily, I was able to exchange it and had the milk and cookies which were delicious! I typically do not like to drink milk, but the milk they serve is magically delicious. 

I also would like to rave about Camille, the server at the bar. She has such a kind and genuine spirit and exudes such grace and positive energy. If you have the chance to be served by her, your experience will be enhanced. 

Overall, Willa Jean is cute, and the food is good enough to come back for a quick bite!
label: positive

text:
Restaurant Week review-

Awesome service on this busy, Wednesday night. When we arrived for our 8:00PM reservation, the host informed us that it will be a 10-15 minute wait. We were able to go upstairs to the bar and enjoy an aperol spritz. Once we got seated, we chose our 4 courses! My husband and I picked different items off the menu, so that we could share - highly recommend! For our first course, we shared the meat and cheese platters. When we combined the two, it was like you had a very fancy, complete charcuterie board. My favorite, to my surprise, was the bologna! For our second course, we chose the arancini and the meatballs. The meatballs were something to write home about... authentic, moist, delicious! The sauce on both were very flavorful. For our third course, we shared the gnocchi and pappardelle. The gnocchi had a square of gelato that gave the gnocchi a slightly sweet, and cold burst to the mushroom sauce. If I had to choose, the pappardelle would be ordered again. And for our last course, gelato! How could you not? Get the stracciatella... you will not be disappointed! Thanks for a great evening.
label: positive

text: Will never set foot in this Pasadena Avenue in South Pasadena Dunkin Donuts again.  We run a non profit Bingo for the senior residents in the area. Was informed that the manager was no longer there...the new manager, with no notice at all, refused to honor the arrangement I had with the former manager, yelled at me while I was standing at the register with other customers behind me, was rude, arrogant and I will NEVER give him my business ever again. text:
I went to NOLA for a while and having eaten so much fried foods, I had needed something that is not as fatty and with a fresh taste. I was pleased to having found a place that serve pho here. 

The place is a family owned and the owner/server was funny. We ordered Pho, a couple tea and juice. 
The pho was pretty decent for the region given that there is not much competition (I think there are only like 4 or 5 Vietnamese spots in the area). It has a refreshing mild taste. 
I thought the price of the pho was fair; however, the Bahn mi was pretty pricey. Bahn mi at this place was similarly priced as the pho. Most places I have been to, Bahn mi at the same location is 20-25% less than pho. 
The juice was definitely not fresh squeeze which is what I expected and they are not falsely advertising. The Vietnamese iced coffee is also overpriced. 

Overall, for the region I think the place is a solid 4 stars and you will not regret trying it. However, If the place was located in any other major cities, I would give them 3 stars ( I just have a very picky taste).
label: positive

text:
I wish you could give two sets of star reviews, but since you cant I will give this a 3.   What I would like to do is give a 1 star and a 5 star.
    
           My 5 star review would be the place, (interior design) and the food.  It is very nice on the inside,I went there for lunch on a weekday because I was treating my elderly neighbor lady, and we wanted to see everything which we did. 
           The food was served hot, fresh, and plated beautifully. My burger was to be cooked med rare, and it was perfect,my neighbor wanted hers med well,and she said it was the best burger she ate.

       The 1 star review is about pricing.  We ordered Gator Bites to start,  then we each had a sportster burger that comes with pub chips, or you can pay for a substitution of fries which we did on each plate. I had a water, she an iced tea. This came out to $49 +change not including tip.
            Seriously?????   I knew it would be a little pricey being a new place, but I can see why it appeared empty. 
                 I am willing to bet a dinner date in the evening while eating off the dinner menu will easily be over $100 not including an alcohol beverage.  Im not knockin this place, just giving my view.  This is a one n done place for me.  
           Oh one thing I did not like was what they do to gouge you a bit more. Burgers come with pub chips for a reason, and thats being people would rather have fries.
           Not only do you pay for a substitutuon, but what they gave you is equivilent to a small portion at any FF restaurant.  We are talking about potatos here. They shouldnt make themselves look bad by being cheap on the fries especially after you over paid for them along with over paying for the burger.  Heck when you go to Five Guys they give you a bunch and thats included.
label: neutral

text:

text:
I used to be a regular at blueprint Delmar, a daily patron and would even attend their special events excitedly. However, at one of their after hours coffee tasting events I was targeted and harassed by a worker who was sexist, believe it or not! 

I sat through the event with a woman friend of mine, and when I went to use the restroom, one of their workers who is also a woman decided to whisper to my friend that she should be wary of me as a person and that she didn't trust me. For some context I had had ZERO negative experiences here prior and had spent many many hours there enjoying time with both men and women friends... so for their worker to had the audacity to impose on my social circuit with their unwarranted suspicion was absolutely astounding to me.

My friend told me that this happened as soon as we left the event. She was very uncomfortable with the fact that the worker chose to say that and quickly reassured me that she didn't have any concerns about my integrity, thank goodness.

So at this point my heart is racing, like borderline anxiety attack, and I'm absolutely confused about why I was targeted and why this had happened after I had spent so much time there, even time ordering from and small talking with this exact worker! I decide to go back in in order to get some clarification and correct the situation, hoping to put my anxiety to rest and also offer some kind of reassurance of my integrity to the person who was suspicious of me.

I calmly walked up to her and said hey I'd like to speak for a second. She immediately dismissed me and said she did not want to talk to me. At this point I literally do start having an anxiety attack and my heart is racing very fast. I kept my tone very calm and maintained courtesy, but I also got very excited internally and said "why would you say something like that to my friend when I went to the bathroom?!" 

She gave me little to no response and just said 'she's seen me talk to women before' (?!) and that she didn't trust me basically. My jaw drops at hearing this because I am very courteous and respectful in all my relations and I anxiously asked her to clarify, but to no avail...

She expressed she felt she had done nothing wrong in saying this to my friend when I walked away. At this point I feel so hurt and so confused and betrayed- I leave shortly after a very short and fruitless exchange with the worker with my heart in my stomach and my anxiety flying sky high.

A few days later I spoke with 2 of the owners there and explained the situation to them and they had absolutely NOTHING to say nor any goal to rectify the situation!! They didn't seem to care in the least and even went so far as to say they were going to 'side with their worker' regardless of the situations specifics and they asked me to not come back while she works there!! At this point I'm offended beyond belief and astounded at the ridiculous judgment call of their owners.

I quit going to blueprint at this point, but the story doesn't entirely end here. Fast forward like a year, maybe 1.5 years, and I walk back in the same store attempting to see if we can clarify and make amends but I don't even get to that point as I can see as soon as I walk in that the same woman who mischievously whispered to my friend was behind the counter and had been promoted to manager! She noticed me immediately and said I wasn't welcome there! It was embarrassing and another big anxiety explosion for me... 

I was hoping the owners would have corrected this, and I even waited a year after the last altercation hoping that one of them would speak with me or offer some clarification since we lived in the same neighborhood and would cross paths now and then. But they've never said a word to me since...

The fact that their owners deemed this appropriate, and felt it was wise to promote this individual, and also felt that making amends with me on a human level wasn't necessary has left a horribly bad taste in my mouth regarding blueprint coffee and I encourage anyone who takes sexism seriously to quit supporting them.
label: negative

text:

text:
Frontier Perk Cafe is something I have wanted to try since it opened. Unfortunately, with their hours being 8a-3p Tuesday through Sunday, it is a bit harder to get there, but I can understand why those hours are needed due to the dedication to the fresh drinks and eats.
Upon walking into the cafe, you are greeted with a warm hello. The cafe itself holds an eclectic but new age feel being in Historic St. Charles. It was clean and had the aroma of fresh coffee being made. The ambiance created due to natural lighting and rustic wood reminded me of being in a peaceful cabin. 

Now, we get to the important part: the food. The front counter had quiche, pastries and meat pies to choose from. What I wanted was no longer on the menu (the lavender latte), but when I asked, they were still able to make it but it was not listed as they are making changes and updating their menu.
I also ordered the bagel and lox that comes with cream cheese, smoked salmon, capers and pickled onion. I ordered a breakfast burrito with potatoes and a cold brew for my husband to go for his lunch later on.
The wait was 10 minutes for everything. Not only did it take no time for my order, everything tasted fresh, the bagel was still warm, and you could tell the pickled onions were made recently because of their crisp and sour punch. The lavender latte was warm, creamy and felt like a hug.
The price point for Frontier Perk is affordable to average, depending on what you get, for the fresh ingredients and dedication to making their food perfect. For 4 items, I was able to only spend $35 (that included a $6 tip).
The only thing I would change about Frontier Perk Cafe is that they were open later so I could enjoy their amazing food any time of the day. 
I will now be recommending this place to anyone I know who is looking for a fantastic and welcoming brunch or lunch spot when visiting the St. Louis/St. Charles area.
label: positive

text:
I finally went to try out this place after seeing all the posts on Instagram.  I called ahead of time on a Saturday just to check that they still had lots of selection.  I was informed that they only make the big tarts in the morning and sell out as the day goes by.  The minis are always available, they are $4.50 each or 6 for $25.00.  Since I was also buying some as a gift, it went for the 6 pack and 2 extra minis.

The tarts look really good however, I think they look better than they taste.  I chose the ones that looked fresh as some flavours were starting to dry out.  There were lots of flavours so it was hard to choose.  The mango was nice, fresh and fruity tasting.  The tiramisu was also nice with that kinda coffee taste.  I was surprised by the crust, it wasn't too hard but looked grainy with a slight beige colour.

There were 3 big tarts available for purchase when I arrived at 5 pm.  I thought they were a bit pricey though.

I won't be running back here to try other flavours but I think the mini box is a great gift idea.  The packaging is cute.
label: neutral

text:
I haven't eaten at a First Watch since I was in Florida a few years ago.  Friend and I went as she's become addicted to the place.  I have to agree the food is usually consistent in quality and quantity.
The place is fairly new. We walked in to a clean place.  The greeter came to us within less than a minute and seated us at the only table left.  Our server was there in a quick moment and took our drink orders.  It was a considerable delay in getting our drinks (the place was  packed) and then our orders were taken.
Friend went with The Works omelet;  I had the Farmhouse Hash, eggs over easy.
Another short delay and the food arrived.  The bowl looked good.  The toast was cold - it had been toasted and then when cool, was slathered with butter.  So it was toast with butter sitting on top of it, not melted in to the bread.  My eggs were not over easy; they were over medium to almost hard and they were almost cold.
I was surprised at the lack of quality in my food.  Given the difficulty in getting help these days I can somewhat understand.  Hence the 4 stars instead of 3.
So the place gets a "meh" this time.
label: positive

text:
As a Queens girl, I was so excited to go to the Mamajuana in Tampa hoping for the same awesomeness from back home but boy was I wrong. The restaurant is beautiful, however that's about it. I ordered calamari (yes I know...who orders that at a Spanish restaurant) as an appetizer and they looked more like onion rings because they were so greasy and fat and were way too chewy and had zero flavor. Our drinks (party of 8) were terrible. Either it tasted like rubbing alcohol or was too watered down to taste anything. 

My friend ordered the chicharrón the pollo app and it definitely did not taste the way it should and it was also way too soft, something just wasn't right. Chicharrón is supposed to be crunchy and tasty and as soon as you bite into it, it takes you back to the motherland...but this took me to a dark alley I never want to walk through. My husbands rice was undercooked which he couldn't even eat and he barely ate the rest of his meal because it just didn't have any taste. I ordered a chicken mofongo with plátano, not the yuca one, and man was it underwhelming. I took three bites and left the rest.  My friend ordered a salad and they said they were out of romaine so the waitress said we have mixed greens instead, friend says yes and receives a plate of spinach only (the only mixing being done was with the DJ). 

Speaking of which, nice touch with the live band, however you couldn't even hear the singers because the base sounded so bad and loud which drowned them out. The option of being able to go to the back lounge portion was nice for a change once you're done not stuffing your face. 

Unfortunately I would definitely not be returning. Not one of us fully ate their meals or even half way, the prices are outrageous (I've eaten better food for way less and didn't have to drive 45 minutes), they just need to do better. If they don't pick up the pace a lot of people are going to be out of a job. Take notes from your fellow NY branches and get some act right in you, otherwise the doors will be closing sooner than you know. Wishing y'all the best but some major changes need to happen.
label: negative

text:

Their gelato flavors rotate, and on this visit I got a crepe topped with saffron white chocolate gelato, which was utterly mesmerizing. But I've tried most of the other regular ones, and you can't go wrong. The smooth, pure flavor stands above most other gelato I've had.

The crepes are quite splendid as well, dusted with sugar and delicate and crisp. Sadly, the magnificent and ludicrously indulgent gelato po-boys I've photographed are no longer on the menu. Until they come back, I think I'm good with the other options.

text:
It was an hour and a half before the restaurant closed, we came in to order two bowls. They only had enough white rice for one bowl and said they were not making any more rice for the day. Are you really telling me at a pokey restaurant you're not gonna make any more fresh rice, and it's an hour and a half before you close??

We had already paid, so we had decided to use the brown rice instead. That was a big mistake, since brown rice on a good day is usually quite dry. I had the chicken bowl, with brown rice and it was not very good. The mangoes were over ripe which really overpowered the whole dish. It was very dry, and the amount of sauce they gave you was not enough with a little ramekin of sauce. I ended up throwing most of it away, which is not something I normally do. I usually would even eat it even if I don't like it, since I don't like to waste food. However it was really not good so I had to throw it away.

My husband had the tuna bowl, and he said it was OK. We probably won't return since the service was not good, the food was not great and it was quite overpriced for what you got.
label: negative

text: overpriced, paid $7 for small coffee that tasted GRAINY and UNTASTEFUL. as quoted by my friend "i would rather drink black coffee with boogers." my other friend got a caramel iced latte, a basic right!? you think that ANY coffee shop could master this....but no! such a disappointment. the ice cubes & aesthetic were the only good things.
label: negative

text:
Impeccable service and delicious food!  Our party of 5 was initially put on a waitlist when we arrived and we were told it would be about a 10 minute wait. Not even 3 minutes later, we get a text saying that our table is ready! The hosts were so patient with our group as a few of them were busy taking photos outside when they called for us 

Our server was also very patient with us as the entire group is vegetarian and we had some modifications because of that. He did not mind and also gave some great recommendations as well! Wish I would've gotten his name so I could give him a shoutout...

The food was delicious and I'd definitely come back! I had the Eggs Florentine with hash browns - it was the perfect serving size as I was able to finish my entire plate for once. 

Definitely recommend giving this place a try for brunch!
label: positive

text: Yummy freshly made pastries (incl gluten free!) as well as nice strong espresso (Caffe D'Arte). Still a bummer masks aren't worn... but if u go during one of their many slower times (hello late afternoon middays ) less of an issue.
label: positive

text:
I LOVED these donuts. I'm not a big donut person because they're often oily / heavy / you can taste that it's been fried in old oil / all of the above. But these donuts were so light and fluffy and all the flavors were delicious. My favorite was the everything donut. Still dreaming about it today 

Would definitely recommend & return
label: positive

text:
They have good coffee and foods!! I like coming here for good coffee but also foods. My favorite is BLTA sandwich and bagels . Their hush browns are really good too. 
I usually park street near the coffee shop but there's parking back of this shop too. Staying there is nice but I guess covid, their windows are open even winter cold days. So it's chilly staying inside.
label: positive

text: Sabrina's has always been a favorite but I have a hard time recommending it now. Waits have always been long, but you can avoid standing outside in the cold with the virtual waitlist or calling ahead. They have stopped these options because of Covid- which makes absolutely no sense as it is contract free and now they are encouraging people congregating while waiting and having to talk directly to a staff member. I just can't justify it, especially with a baby out in the cold, and I know other people feel the same. Happy to change my review it at least reconsider if they can rationalize this decision.
label: negative

text: This was the worst Kung Pao I've ever had. Only a couple pieces of super dry over cooked chicken with 1000 green onions (even the bottoms) and bamboo shoots with absolutely zero spice. How this is Kung Pao is beyond me.... Also ordered sweet and sour chicken which was fine but it comes with sauce already on it. My son wouldn't touch it. Wish it said this on the menu.
label: negative

text: Got the legendairy free milkshake and waited for 45 min. The toppings were fine but the milkshake was TERRIBLE! it was flavorless, watery, and had a very weird consistency! very underwhelming and expensive! the inside of the location looks run down and the fluorescent lights will suck out your soul while you wait and wait and wait to get your shake. I don't usually dine at places just for food that looks pretty so overall we were very disappointed in the taste of the shake, the service, the price, and the atmosphere. we thought we would love legendairy but we will not be returning.
label: negative

text: Excellent food prepared by executive chef Federick. Will be back again soon for family reunion dinner
label: positive

text: the lady running the drive through cut me off about 10 times while i was trying to order, and talked over me while i was explaining what i want, then tried to tel me i ordered two iced teas. I ordered two iced coffees, but she didn't hear me say with cream and sugar because she wouldn't shut up. She kept rushing me through ordering, and gave me sausage egg and cheese wraps instead of egg and cheese. the only correct item i got was my order of hash browns, and she had the nerve to argue that I ordered two iced teas. Like what?? I've come here almost every day for the last month and i've never seen this woman here, and I hope she either gets fired or never works the drive-through again
label: negative

text: I recommend this location as they r pretty consistent & most importantly always clean- inside & out- incl bathrooms. I really value that I can count on Starbucks standards at this store. Also- the staff is almost always super friendly! I do wish the bike rack was located closer-
label: positive

text:
I live in West Philly, but whenever I can I will walk to Rival Bros for my decaf latte with almond milk. Whether iced or hot Rival knows how to make this drink right. I always complain how I cannot find a good cup of latte across the river in the West Philly side. Don't misunderstand me, there are enough cafes, but somehow a lot of the coffee based drinks are either too strong or too weak. 
Rival Bros seem to get the flavour just right. The service has been good and steady during this pandemic, and the place is always clean. It gets A+ on my cafe list!
label: positive

text: It's Starbucks. My default rating for fast food chains is 3 stars, and they didn't do anything special to rise above that. They didn't do anything bad enough to lose a star either, though I was seriously tempted to drop one for the lack of drive-thru. There is at least a parking lot though.
label: neutral

text:
The last couple of times I've been here I haven't been impressed. Both times, I ordered a flavored lemonade, the last time, it was the pina Colada lemonade. There wasn't any flavored added, very bland. I'd say it was a one off, but I think they always forget or very stingy with the flavorings. It also takes forever to get the drink, very slow service. For the price you pay for a lemonade, you'd think it would be amazing and good service. 
I also wanted to try the flights since there are so many options but they don't offer them if you go through the drive thru. This is very disappointing since we are in a pandemic and all. There could at least be communication on the drive thru sign about what they refuse to serve. I'm disappointed and hope they improve.
label: negative

text: Didn't realize how much I'd missed coffee shops like this!
label: positive

text: Quaint cute and delicious. Excellent for breakfast too. Great ambiance. Highly recommend
label: positive

text: My husband and I love coming here for their chicken & waffles . There's never usually a wait . Wait staff is always friendly . The food is amazing and reasonably priced and the owner is so kind. He has come out to check on the food and say hi every time we have been there . I couldn't recommend this place enough if you're in the area and looking for something good to eat !
label: positive

text: My friend and I tried a honey lavender donut and a salted honey pie. Both were DELISH!
label: positive

text:
First of all, the place is gorgeous!!!! It's so relaxing to sit in there and take in the wonderful atmosphere. It truly feels like a home away from home. The new design of the place is astonishing and so pleasing to lounge in. I love bringing my friends and family here for an enjoyable outing. 

 The drinks & food are delectable and crafted with precision and care. I adore trying new items from their menu and everytime I am genuinely satisfied! The staff are always extremely accommodating and friendly. 

 Overall, it's a really great place to visit and feel comfortable while enjoying something delicious. Plus, the new renovations are beyond beautiful and absolutely worth checking out. You'll be stunned with just how cozy and happy this place is.
label: positive

text: I cannot say enough good things about this new business! All of the staff was very welcoming. The inside of the cafe is small and quaint! I love that there is optional seating upstairs if you would like. Lovely place to hangout and spend time with cats while enjoying a drink from their cafe. Definitely will be back!
label: positive

text: Never disappoints! Love their breakfast and their chai tea. Avocado toast is simply the best around.
label: positive

text: 10:40PM on a Friday and denied service due to "staff shortage"... poor management, period. Sad excuse for a company after speaking with corporate and them basically saying "there's nothing we can do." Uhhh hire people?? Maybe that location can't retain employees due to such poor management, get it together. I'm sure it's not their fault but if other convenience stores function fine with 2 employees I will just take my business there honestly just close that location down if you can't "staff it" properly. Avoid this location at all costs other reviews are not wrong!
label: negative

text: Very friendly service, our server Nora  was a pure pleasure .good ambiance and a fresh take on breakfast
label: positive

text: Enjoyed a tea with a friend over the holidays. We did the winter pre-fixe menu and it was great. I think I'd prefer to return for tea sandwiches in the future. They didn't rush you at all. I thoroughly enjoyed our experience.
label: positive

text: This place was good! I got a BLT and added turkey. It was quite delicious but too ezpensive for what it was. I spent almost $30 for the sandwich + tip. I also didn't love the peppered chips, but I will still give this good scoring because the bread, meat, and everything in between was very good. Service was great, and speedy too.
label: positive

text: Nice cafe in Old City, good for working as they have free wifi and it is not too loud besides the lunch rush. I had an apple brie sandwich which came out toasted. The sandwich was a good blend of sweet and crunchy from the apples and cheesy from the brie.
label: positive

text: Awesome service the employees were extremely nice and the food was delicious, honestly this is the best location to my experience in central Florida.
label: positive

text: Great hours with a lot of out door space, for those need to do extreme Covid-19 distancing. Great selection of coffee and non-caffeinated drink (alcohol is available), and snacks.
label: positive
